name: sanic-jwt
documentation:
# Sanic JWT

[![Latest PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/sanic-jwt.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sanic-jwt)
[![Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/sanic-jwt.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sanic-jwt)
[![Version status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/sanic-jwt.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sanic-jwt)
[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/sanic-jwt.svg)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ahopkins/sanic-jwt/dev/LICENSE)

[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/ahopkins/sanic-jwt.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/ahopkins/sanic-jwt)
[![Documentation](https://readthedocs.org/projects/sanic-jwt/badge/?version=latest)](http://sanic-jwt.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
[![Codacy Badge](https://api.codacy.com/project/badge/Grade/9727756ffccd45f7bc5ad6292596e03d)](https://www.codacy.com/app/ahopkins/sanic-jwt?utm_source=github.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=ahopkins/sanic-jwt&utm_campaign=Badge_Grade)
[![Test Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/ahopkins/sanic-jwt/branch/dev/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/ahopkins/sanic-jwt)
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/ambv/black)


Sanic JWT adds authentication protection and endpoints to [Sanic].

It is both **easy** to get up and running, and **extensible** for the
developer. It can act to **protect endpoints** and also provide **authentication scoping**, all wrapped into a nice [JWT].

[Read the documentation](http://sanic-jwt.rtfd.io/) | [View the source code](https://github.com/ahopkins/sanic-jwt/)

------

**What do I do?**

It's easy: (1) install, (2) initialize, and (3) authenticate.

**Install:**

```
pip install sanic-jwt
```

  [Sanic]: http://sanic.readthedocs.io
  [JWT]: https://jwt.io

**Initialize:**

```python
from sanic import Sanic
from sanic_jwt import Initialize

def my_authenticate(request, *args, **kwargs):
    ...

app = Sanic()
Initialize(
    app,
    authenticate=my_authenticate
)
```

**Authenticate:**

    http://localhost/auth

------

**Can I customize it?**

Definitely! Sanic JWT is made to allow developers to customize the operation to fit their needs. [Check out the documentation](http://sanic-jwt.rtfd.io/) to learn how.

functions.name: sanic_jwt.validators.normalize
documentation: Normalizes and returns tuple consisting of namespace, and action(s)

functions.name: sanic_jwt.utils.algorithm_is_asymmetric
documentation:
This is a simple method to verify the need to provide a private key to
a given `algorithm`, as [documented by PyJWT](https://pyjwt.readthedocs.io/en/latest/algorithms.html)

```
param algorithm

the given algorithm, like HS256, ES384, RS512, PS256, etc

return

True if algorithm is asymmetric
```

methods.name: sanic_jwt.configuration.Configuration.get
documentation: Helper method to avoid calling getattr

methods.name: sanic_jwt.configuration.Configuration.extract_presets
documentation: Pull the application's configurations for Sanic JWT

functions.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.initialize
documentation:
Functional approach to initializing Sanic JWT. This was the original
method, but was replaced by the Initialize class. It is recommended to use
the class because it is more flexible. There is no current plan to remove
this method, but it may be depracated in the future.

classes.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__check_deprecated
documentation: Checks for deprecated configuration keys

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__add_endpoints
documentation: Initialize the Sanic JWT Blueprint and add to the instance initialized

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__add_class_views
documentation: Include any custom class views on the Sanic JWT Blueprint

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__check_classes
documentation:
Check if any of the default classes (`Authentication`, `Configuration`
and / or `Responses`) have been overwitten and if they're still valid

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__initialize_instance
documentation: Take any predefined methods/handlers and insert them into Sanic JWT

methods.name: sanic_jwt.initialization.Initialize.__load_configuration
documentation:
Configure settings for the instance in the following order:

1. Sanic JWT's defaults
2. Custom Configuration class
3. Key word arguments passed to Initialize

Class used to initialize Sanic JWT

Must be initialized with a keyword argument: `authenticate` that is a
method that MUST return a user object that when iterated upon returns a
dictionary, or has a `to_dict` method. The resulting dictionary MUST
have a key/value for a unique user id.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.BaseAuthentication._get_user_id
documentation:
Get a user_id from a user object. If `asdict` is True, will return
it as a dict with `config.user_id` as key. The `asdict` keyword
defaults to `False`.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.BaseAuthentication.add_claims
documentation:
Injects standard claims into the payload for: exp, iss, iat, nbf, aud.
And, custom claims, if they exist

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._check_authentication
documentation:
Checks a request object to determine if that request contains a valid,
and authenticated JWT.

It returns a tuple:
1. Boolean whether the request is authenticated with a valid JWT
2. HTTP status code
3. Reasons (if any) for a potential authentication failure

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._decode
documentation:
Take a JWT and return a decoded payload. Optionally, will verify
the claims on the token.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_payload
documentation: Given a user object, create a payload and extend it as configured.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_refresh_token
documentation: Extract a refresh token from a request object.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_token_from_cookies
documentation: Extract the token if present inside the request cookies.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_token_from_headers
documentation: Extract the token if present inside the headers of a request.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_token_from_query_string
documentation: Extract the token if present from the request args.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._get_token
documentation: Extract a token from a request object.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication._verify
documentation: Verify that a request object is authenticated.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.extract_payload
documentation: Extract a payload from a request object.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.extract_scopes
documentation: Extract scopes from a request object.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.extract_user_id
documentation: Extract a user id from a request object.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.generate_access_token
documentation: Generate an access token for a given user.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.generate_refresh_token
documentation: Generate a refresh token for a given user.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.is_authenticated
documentation:
Checks a request object to determine if that request contains a valid,
and authenticated JWT.

methods.name: sanic_jwt.authentication.Authentication.verify_token
documentation: Perform an inline verification of a token.
